demographic_audit_note: Audited Atlantis queue against facility face sheet; corrected DOB from 05/12/1954 to 05/12/1955. Confirmed Medicare Advantage PPO eligibility and verified home address in the 19104 zip code for MA travel distance.
home_vs_ltc_determination: Social Worker Maria confirmed the goal is Home with HHA services for daily diabetic wound dressing changes and PT for gait training.
weekly_facility_update: Week 2: Wound healing is stable; SW confirms target discharge for Friday. Patient remains a high fall risk, requiring a hospital bed and O2 concentrator at home before transition.
v_card_and_flyer_status: PN V-Card successfully inserted into patient's contact list; physical program flyer delivered to the patient's daughter during the bedside education session.
pre_dc_pulse_call_result: Reached daughter via phone 24 hours prior to discharge. Daughter reported that the DME vendor delivered a standard manual bed instead of the prescribed semi-electric model with a pressure-reduction mattress, creating a safety risk for the patient's diabetic ulcers.
atlantis_final_sync: Entered D/C date of 10/27/2023 and 1st Visit Date of 10/28/2023 into Atlantis after SW confirmed the DME swap was expedited.
ma_visit_booking: Medical Assistant scheduled for 10/28/2023 at 11:00 AM, following the HHA's confirmed Start of Care window to ensure clinical continuity.
narrative_summary: In Stage 1, I audited demographic errors in Atlantis and confirmed the home discharge goal with the SW. During Stage 2, I maintained weekly check-ins and educated the family, which proved critical when the daughter identified a DME delivery failure during my 24-hour pulse call. I successfully escalated the equipment mismatch to the SW, allowing for a corrected delivery before finalizing the Stage 3 handoff and scheduling the MA first visit.

format_1_state_log.event_description: Initial Atlantis Triage and Demographic Audit
DME Vendor delivers manual bed instead of semi-electric model
SW coordinates expedited equipment swap; PN verifies delivery at home

format_2_triples.situation: Daughter reports wrong bed type arrived at the residence during pulse call.
format_2_triples.action_taken: Immediately flagged the specific equipment mismatch to the SW to prevent an unsafe discharge.

format_2_triples.situation: Patient's daughter is unsure how to handle the first 24 hours post-discharge.
format_2_triples.action_taken: Walked the family through the Day 1 checklist, including MA arrival time and wound care supply verification.

format_2_triples.situation: Atlantis record shows an outdated phone number for the primary caregiver.
format_2_triples.action_taken: Updated the Atlantis demographic fields with the daughter's current mobile number after verifying the face sheet.

format_3_rl_scenario.description: Wait for the Social Worker to receive confirmation from the DME vendor regarding the replacement bed delivery before updating the Atlantis transition record.
format_3_rl_scenario.rationale: This follows the SW boundary by allowing the facility professional to manage the logistics of the equipment vendor while the PN remains in a monitoring stance for the final handoff.

format_3_rl_scenario.description: Verify the specific delivery window for the replacement bed with the family and ensure the MA visit is scheduled only after the equipment is confirmed to be in place.
format_3_rl_scenario.rationale: This aligns with the Stage 3 Scheduling Anchor, ensuring the environment is safe before the 'First Visit' incentive is triggered.

format_3_rl_scenario.description: Call the DME company directly to demand an upgrade to a higher-tier air mattress and suggest the SW switch to a different vendor that provides faster service.
format_3_rl_scenario.rationale: This is an overstep because it involves managing vendor relationships and suggesting specific providers, which falls under the SW's exclusive domain.
